definition: to walk with big strong steps
example: The soldiers march in a straight line.

definition: to run very fast
example: He dashed to the door before it closed.

definition: to run after something
example: The dog liked to chase the ball in the yard.

definition: to take something quickly with your hand
example: She tried to grab the last cookie before her brother.

definition: to throw something gently
example: He would toss the ball to his friend.

definition: to take hold of something in the air
example: She held out her hands to catch the ball.

definition: to press something tight
example: She squeezed the sponge to get the water out.

definition: to make your body long
example: The cat liked to stretch after a nap.

definition: to make something not straight
example: You need to bend down to pick up the toy.

definition: to turn something around
example: She had to twist the cap to open the bottle.

definition: to move something back and forth fast
example: He would shake the box to guess what was inside.

definition: to move something around in a circle
example: She used a spoon to stir the soup.

definition: to make water or juice flow out of something
example: Please pour some milk into my cup.

definition: to let water or juice fall by accident
example: Be careful not to spill your juice on the table.

definition: to fall in small drops, one at a time
example: Water started to drip from the leaky roof.

definition: to make water fly everywhere
example: The kids love to splash in the pool.

definition: to stay on top of water
example: The rubber duck can float in the bathtub.

definition: to go down under the water
example: The heavy rock will sink to the bottom.

definition: to change from hard to soft, like ice becoming water
example: The ice cream will melt if you leave it in the sun.

definition: to get so cold it turns to ice
example: The water will freeze into ice tonight.

definition: to take the skin off a fruit
example: She had to peel the banana before eating it.

definition: to cut into pieces
example: Dad helped chop the vegetables for dinner.

definition: to rub food on a tool to make tiny pieces
example: He helped grate the cheese for the pizza.

definition: to put a thin layer on top
example: She liked to spread butter on her toast.

definition: to drop tiny bits on something
example: He wanted to sprinkle sugar on his cereal.

definition: to pick up with a spoon or hands
example: She used a big spoon to scoop the ice cream.

definition: to speak in a soft low voice
example: She had to whisper, so the baby would not wake up.

definition: to say something very loud
example: He had to shout, so his friend across the field could hear.

definition: to laugh in a quiet silly way
example: The joke made him giggle and cover his mouth.

definition: to make a long loud cry
example: The wolf would howl at the moon every night.

definition: the outer covering of a tree; also the loud sound a dog makes
example: The bark of the old oak tree was rough and bumpy.

definition: to make a big loud sound like a lion
example: The lion opened its mouth to roar.

definition: to make music with your mouth closed
example: She liked to hum her favorite song while she worked.

definition: to hit your hands together
example: Everyone began to clap when the show ended.

definition: to move your hand to say hello or bye; also a wall of water in the ocean
example: She would wave goodbye from the window.

definition: to move your head up and down for yes
example: He nodded to show he understood.

definition: to look quickly or secretly
example: She tried to peek at her birthday present.

definition: to look at something for a long time
example: He could not stop staring at the huge cake.

definition: to look at something very quickly
example: She glanced at the clock and ran to catch the bus.

definition: to look carefully for something
example: He had to search everywhere for his lost toy.

definition: to find or learn about something for the first time
example: She was excited to discover a frog under the log.

definition: to see or become aware of something
example: Did you notice the bird sitting on the fence?

definition: to think about something you don't know
example: I wonder what is inside that big box.

definition: to make pictures in your mind
example: Close your eyes and imagine you are flying.

definition: to act like something that is not real
example: Let's pretend we are pirates on a ship!
imageKeyword: child pretending to be superhero cape

definition: to say you will really do something
example: I promise I will clean my room after dinner.

definition: to help someone recall
example: Please remind me to bring my book tomorrow.

definition: to not recall something
example: Don't forget to pack your lunch!

definition: to be in the right place
example: These books belong on the top shelf.
imageKeyword: toy in a box with name tag

definition: to give part of yours to someone
example: She learned to share her toys with her sister.

definition: to give one thing and get a different thing back
example: I will trade my apple for your orange.

definition: to use something and give it back
example: Can I borrow your pencil? I will return it soon.

definition: to let someone use your thing for a short time
example: She was kind to lend me her umbrella.

definition: to pick up and bring together
example: Let's gather sticks to build a fort.

definition: to get many of the same thing
example: He liked to collect shiny rocks from the beach.

definition: to put things on top of each other neatly
example: He liked to stack the blocks as high as he could.

definition: to cover something with paper or cloth
example: She helped wrap the gift in pretty paper.

definition: to take the cover off something
example: He was so excited to unwrap his birthday present.

definition: to pull hard on something
example: She had to tug the door hard to open it.

definition: to pull something heavy along the ground
example: He had to drag the heavy bag across the floor.

definition: to push hard
example: The wind would shove the door shut.

definition: to push edges in to make neat
example: Mom would tuck the blanket around him at bedtime.

definition: to put something up so it is not on the ground
example: Please hang your coat on the hook.

definition: to close or attach something
example: Please fasten your seatbelt before the car moves.

definition: to join one thing to another
example: He used a clip to attach the paper to the board.

definition: to fix something broken
example: Dad can repair the broken chair with glue.

definition: to make something new
example: She used paint to create a beautiful picture.

definition: to plan what something will look like
example: He wanted to design his own treehouse.

definition: to find out how big something is
example: She used a ruler to measure how tall the plant grew.

definition: to find out how heavy something is
example: Let's weigh the pumpkin to see how heavy it is.

definition: to say numbers to find how many
example: Can you count how many apples are in the basket?

definition: to put things in groups
example: She helped sort the socks by color.

definition: to find two things that are the same; also a game between two sides
example: Can you match the baby animal to its father?

definition: to bring something to someone
example: The mail carrier will deliver the package today.

definition: to go get something and bring it back
example: The dog ran to fetch the stick I threw.

definition: to go away so no one can see it, like magic
example: The magician made the coin vanish!

definition: so small you can barely see it or hold it
example: The tiny ant carried a crumb ten times its size.

definition: so big it fills up a room or towers over you
example: The huge elephant was taller than the bus.

definition: so big it is hard to believe
example: The enormous whale made the boat look like a toy.
imageKeyword: enormous whale next to small boat

definition: not very wide
example: The path was so narrow only one person could walk at a time.

definition: going far from side to side
example: The river was too wide to jump across.

definition: going up very sharply, hard to climb
example: The hill was so steep they had to crawl up it.

definition: not deep
example: The water was shallow enough for the little kids to stand in.

definition: going far down
example: The pool was too deep for her to touch the bottom.

definition: not thin; having a lot from one side to the other
example: The thick book had over 300 pages.

definition: not thick; having very little from one side to the other
example: The ice was too thin to walk on safely.

definition: not bumpy at all
example: The rock was smooth like a piece of glass.

definition: bumpy and not smooth
example: The tree bark felt rough under her fingers.

definition: with a point that can cut
example: Be careful! That knife is very sharp.

definition: not sharp at all
example: The dull scissors could not cut the paper.

definition: bright and reflecting light
example: The shiny coin sparkled in the sunlight.

definition: a little bit wet
example: The grass was damp from the morning dew.

definition: very very wet all over
example: He was soaking wet after playing in the rain.

definition: not wet at all
example: She waited for her hair to get dry after her bath.

definition: something that holds on to your fingers
example: His hands were sticky from the candy.

definition: wet and slippery in a yucky way
example: The slimy slug left a trail on the sidewalk.

definition: soft and light like a cloud
example: The fluffy kitten looked like a little cotton ball.

definition: covered in soft short hair
example: The fuzzy peach tickled his lip.

definition: warm and comfortable
example: The cozy bed made her want to stay asleep.

definition: a little cold
example: The chilly wind made her wish she had a jacket.

definition: so cold like ice
example: The water was freezing so no one wanted to swim.

definition: so hot with lots of bubbles
example: The boiling water was too hot to touch.

definition: a nice heat that feels good
example: The warm sun felt nice on her face.

definition: very strong and scary, ready to attack
example: The fierce lion roared and showed its sharp teeth.

definition: soft and careful
example: He was gentle when he held the baby bird.

definition: not afraid to do scary things
example: The brave knight went into the dark cave.

definition: afraid to talk to new people
example: The shy girl hid behind her mother.

definition: happy about something you did well
example: She felt proud when she finished the hard puzzle.

definition: wanting to know about things
example: The curious cat peeked inside the open box.

definition: in a bad mood
example: He was grumpy because he did not sleep well.

definition: happy and smiling
example: The cheerful girl sang a song on the way to school.

definition: sad because you are alone
example: The lonely puppy sat by the door waiting for someone to play.

definition: quiet and not upset
example: The lake was calm and still like a mirror.

definition: not tame, living free in nature
example: The wild horse ran free across the plains.

definition: friendly and used to people
example: The tame rabbit let the children pet it.

definition: not fancy, with nothing on it
example: He wanted a plain white T-shirt with no picture.

definition: having a lot of pretty things on it
example: She wore a fancy dress with sparkles to the party.

definition: ready to eat (for fruit)
example: The ripe banana was yellow and sweet.

definition: old and bad to eat
example: The rotten apple was brown and smelled terrible.

definition: just made or picked, not old
example: The fresh bread smelled wonderful from the oven.

definition: old and not fresh anymore
example: The stale crackers were not crunchy anymore.

definition: a strong taste that is not sweet
example: The dark chocolate tasted bitter to her.

definition: a sharp taste like lemon
example: The sour candy made her face scrunch up.

definition: tasting like salt
example: The ocean water was too salty to drink.

definition: full of juice inside
example: The juicy orange dripped down his chin.

definition: making a snapping sound when you bite
example: The crunchy carrot snapped when she bit it.

definition: smooth and soft like cream
example: The creamy soup was thick and warm.

definition: making no sound at all
example: The house was silent because everyone was asleep.

definition: making a lot of noise
example: The loud music made it hard to talk.

definition: having a hole or empty space inside
example: The hollow log made a drum sound when she hit it.

definition: hard and firm; keeps its shape
example: Ice is solid, but it melts into water.

definition: not tight
example: His loose tooth wiggled when he touched it.

definition: fitting very close, hard to move
example: The lid was on so tight he could not open the jar.

definition: not straight, bent
example: The old fence was crooked and leaning to one side.

definition: going one way without bending
example: She drew a straight line with her ruler.

definition: full of too many people or things
example: The bus was so crowded there was no place to sit.

definition: with nothing inside
example: The empty jar had no cookies left.

definition: all of it, nothing missing
example: He ate the whole pizza by himself!

definition: extra, in case you need it
example: Mom keeps a spare key under the mat.

definition: you know it is true for sure
example: I am certain I left my bag on the chair.

definition: odd or not like what you thought
example: She heard a strange noise coming from the closet.

definition: very good and nice
example: The birthday party was wonderful!

definition: so bad it makes you upset or scared
example: The terrible storm knocked down many trees.

definition: nothing wrong at all
example: She drew a perfect circle without any bumps.

definition: not nice to look at
example: The ugly duckling grew into a beautiful swan.

definition: so lovely it makes you stop and stare
example: The sunset painted the sky in beautiful colors.

definition: smart and good at thinking
example: The clever fox found a way out of the trap.

definition: not smart, silly in a bad way
example: It was foolish to go outside without a coat in the snow.
imageKeyword: person walking in rain no umbrella

definition: wanting too much for yourself
example: The greedy boy took all the cookies and shared none.

definition: happy to give to others
example: He was generous and shared his lunch with his friend.

definition: able to wait and stay calm
example: Be patient — your turn will come soon.

definition: will not change your mind
example: The stubborn mule would not move no matter what.

definition: not wanting to work or move
example: The lazy cat slept all day in the sunny spot.

definition: doing many things with no free time
example: The bees were very busy making honey.

definition: bumping into things a lot
example: The clumsy puppy tripped over its own feet.

definition: moving in a pretty smooth way
example: The graceful dancer spun without falling.

definition: in a fast way
example: She quickly grabbed her bag and ran to school.

definition: in a not-fast way
example: The turtle walked slowly across the road.

definition: without making noise
example: She quietly closed the door, so the baby would not wake.

definition: with a lot of noise
example: The baby cried loudly in the middle of the night.

definition: in a soft careful way
example: She gently placed the egg in the basket.

definition: quickly and without warning
example: Suddenly the lights went out, and the room was dark.

definition: before now; earlier than expected
example: She was surprised he had already finished his homework.

definition: very close but not quite
example: He almost caught the ball, but it slipped away.

definition: only just, by a tiny bit
example: He could barely reach the top shelf on his toes.

definition: maybe, it might happen
example: Perhaps we will go to the park if it stops raining.

definition: just right; not more or less
example: That is exactly what I was looking for!

definition: in place of something else
example: It rained, so we played inside instead.

definition: even so, no matter what
example: It was raining, but she went outside anyway.

definition: for all time, never ending
example: I wish summer could last forever.

definition: away from each other
example: The two friends lived far apart but still wrote letters.

definition: with each other
example: The kids played together in the sandbox.

definition: toward the front
example: Take one step forward and two steps back.

definition: toward the back
example: She counted backward from ten to one.

definition: to the left or right
example: The crab walked sideways across the sand.

definition: right below it
example: The cat hid beneath the bed.

definition: higher than something
example: The bird flew above the trees.

definition: lower than something
example: The fish swam below the boat.

definition: next to
example: She sat beside her best friend.

definition: in the middle of two things
example: The ball rolled between the two chairs.

definition: in the middle of many things
example: He found his toy among all the others in the box.
imageKeyword: toy in box full of toys

definition: in the direction of
example: The puppy ran toward its owner.

definition: touching and leaning on
example: He leaned against the wall and waited for his friend.

definition: going in one end and out the other
example: The train went through the long tunnel.

definition: from this side over to that side
example: She swam across the pool to the other end.

definition: following the edge or line of
example: They walked along the path by the river.

definition: in a circle
example: The dog ran around the tree three times.

definition: past, farther away than
example: The park is just beyond those tall trees.

definition: at the same time as something else
example: She fell asleep during the movie.

definition: up to the time that
example: Wait here until I come back.

definition: from that time to now
example: She has been happy since she got the puppy.

definition: if one thing or another
example: I don't know whether to bring a coat or not.
imageKeyword: child holding coat looking at sky

definition: at the same time as
example: She read a book while she waited for the bus.

definition: in addition to that, also
example: Besides apples, she also likes grapes.

definition: inside
example: Stay within the lines when you color.

definition: not having
example: He went to school without his lunch box.

definition: in every part of
example: Flowers grew throughout the whole garden.
imageKeyword: flowers in every part of garden

definition: on top of (used in stories)
example: Once upon a time, there was a little bear.

definition: to lift something from the ground
example: Please pick up your toys from the floor.

definition: to set something on a table or the floor
example: She put down the heavy bag on the table.
